    • Full scRNA-seq Workflow

Generate a named color vector from a variable

SelectColors.Rd

Accepts any vector (factor or not), resolves its levels, and returns a named character vector of colors - one per level - ready to pass to scale_color_manual(), PrepObject(custom_colors = ...), or any scSidekick colors argument.

Usage

SelectColors(x, palette = "all", levels = NULL, reverse = FALSE)

Arguments

x

A vector (factor, character, or numeric).

palette

Color source. One of:

  • A Nour_pal name string (e.g. "all", "spectrum").

  • A character vector of hex colors - interpolated to the required length via colorRampPalette.

  • A palette function that accepts an integer n and returns n colors (e.g. scales::hue_pal()).

Default "all".

levels

Optional character vector specifying level order. Levels present in x but absent here are appended at the end.

reverse

Logical. Reverse the color order. Default FALSE.

Value

A named character vector of hex colors, one per level.

Details

If x is not already a factor it is converted automatically and a message is printed. Supply levels to control the order; any levels present in the data but not listed are appended at the end.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# From a Seurat metadata column
cols <- SelectColors(SeuratObj$seurat_clusters)

# Custom level order + warm palette
cols <- SelectColors(SeuratObj$Group,
  levels  = c("Control", "Treated"),
  palette = "warm")

# Any color vector as palette
cols <- SelectColors(SeuratObj$CellType,
  palette = c("#003f5c", "#bc5090", "#ffa600"))
} # }
